summary.intent: Complete a multi-pass Codex review cycle for PR #678 on fogodev/ars-ui by resolving all review threads in the splitter component, iterating on fixes until Codex approves.
summary.outcome: Three rounds of Codex review were completed; fixes for splitter controlled sizing, drag state cleanup, collapse behavior, and keyboard handling were committed and pushed (commits 5bc69730 and a follow-up), with all review threads replied to and resolved.

repo: The splitter component lives at crates/ars-components/src/layout/splitter.rs with a companion spec at spec/components/layout/splitter.md that must be kept in sync with implementation changes.
The repo uses cargo xtask for spec tooling (cargo xtask spec context splitter, cargo xtask spec validate) and cargo insta for snapshot testing.
Codex review is triggered by posting '@codex review' as a PR comment and tracked via GitHub reactions (👀 = reviewing, 👍 = approved) and unresolved review threads.
auto_columns(Some(...)) is expected to clear the default fixed column count; columns(Some(...)) can still restore explicit columns afterward — these are distinct APIs with different semantics.
SyncProps must exit Dragging state and clear drag bookkeeping when the active handle becomes invalid after prop changes.
collapse() must always move the target panel to collapsed_size while only capping the amount transferred into a max-constrained neighbor, not the target itself.
Splitter keymap covers Home, End, Enter, Space, and arrow keys — all must be handled in the Dragging KeyDown path.

code.finding: Controlled sizing commits must be observable through the bindable value; uncontrolled initial sizes must be clamped at initialization.
Spec snippets describing controlled sizing and uncontrolled initial size clamping paths were updated to match the new implementation contract.
Spec sections covering clamp_all, valid_handle, collapse_panel, and keyboard key handling were reviewed and patched.

workflow: Codex review polling strategy: check GitHub reactions for 👀 (in progress) vs 👍 (approved), and count unresolved codex-authored threads; poll every 120 seconds.
The pattern for resolving Codex threads: post a reply citing the fix commit hash with a brief description, then resolve the thread via the GraphQL resolveReviewThread mutation in one batched script.
Full check suite before each commit: cargo fmt, focused unit tests, spec conformance tests, proptest state machines (PROPTEST_CASES=256), and insta snapshot tests with --unreferenced=reject.
git commit --no-gpg-sign is used in this repo's automated flow, with Co-authored-by Codex trailers.

friction: Codex third pass took ~10+ minutes with no visible thread activity while showing 👀, requiring several extra polling ticks and a defensive re-check before results appeared.
Multiple apply_patch/fmt/test cycles were needed for the third-pass fixes, suggesting the fixes required iteration to get right.
The polling loop had to handle edge cases where threads count could be undefined (${threads:-0} guard needed).
open_items: Awaiting final Codex 👍 reaction to confirm the third-pass fixes are fully accepted — the transcript ends before approval is confirmed.
Proptest state machine coverage was run at PROPTEST_CASES=256 with --ignored flag, suggesting these are heavier tests not run by default that may need periodic re-evaluation of case counts.
